channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: You should be holding at least 1 BTC. — 122 replies — 98.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
You should be holding at least 1 BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 122 • Updated: 2026-05-22 10:15 UTC

OP

Imagine not owning at least 1 BTC before institutions and governments vacuum the remaining supply while you’re still debating if Bitcoin is a scam… or just trading crypto without considering BTC long term.

Early replies (first 3)

62219945
>>62219942
Or I can trade alts (leveraged BTC) and actually make it instead of getting a 2x pump

62219995
Fuck. I have 2BTC . Is that enough bro?

62220027
>>62219995
no, i have 3. and it's not enough, we're just poor faggots

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62239105
>just buy into the obvious ponzi scheme bro

62240786
>>62228616
hmm find better opportunities

62241339
>>62219942
Soon I will get to that 1 BTC mark, RN I'm buying more and staking some.

62244007
>>62238430
That's the right move anon

62244072
>>62219942
Bitcoin is getting fucked by the Jews

62246429
>>62241339
I know you can do it anon

62246430
>>62239105
If it has a high likelihood to make me rich then I dont care

62246437
>>62244072
it's just the cycle

62246447
>>62219942
I don't want to buy btc

62246571
>>62221733
>Its ok. I have monkey NFTs. They are the future
It still blows my mind that people thought rare Pepes could actually be a real thing.

62248268
>>62244072
Including you?

62248275
>>62222594
Bad actors are not going to have access to a quantum computer before things get figured out.

62249623
>>62246447
No problem lol

62251167
>>62246571
I think he's just being sarcastic lol

62252826
>>62219942
based
>t. living in (((hyperinflation)))
read about monero too
its the final solution to the taxman question and makes keynesian jews seethe

62252834
>>62219942
I mined 24 bitcoin on a RX480 in 2011 and I still have them.

62252845
>>62252834
truly based if true

62252856

and bankrupt our central banks
if I didn't need the cash and had to sell for fiat I would have a much needed two thousand fucking dollars right now
keep stacking brothers
death to fiat

62253066
>>62252834
RX480 was released in 2016 you LARPer

62253216
>>62252856
Crazy I'm from lebanon too and someone didn't send me any btc because I'm not a serial begger, I too could use a very much needed 2k right now crazy!

62253224
>>62252856
This guys an israeli scammer btw he just makes scams out of any trend clearly he struck it big in 2020 as he said someone sent him multiple bitcoins and he's still here pretending.
He know he isn't from Lebanon but his location came up Florida multiple times when the sharty hack was released he was a trial janitor here.

62253228
>>62252856
He's also pretending his sister killed herself and asking for money for it in XMR threads.
Last it was his dad that died but only after he got 8k for it.
Fucker has probably got 7 figures

62254644
>>62253228
8K?
nigger I would fucking beat you up just because you're that spiteful
I never claimed I had a sister
>>62253216
iza 3anjad e7ki 3arabe yalla
>>62253224
>He know he isn't from Lebanon but his location came up Florida multiple times when the sharty hack was released he was a trial janitor here.
kek
I posted proof so many times
seethe more jew

62254676
>>62253224
>multiple bitcoins
>multiple bitcoins
>multiple bitcoins
how dense are you
I even showed the screencap it was like 300$ total

62255610
>>62254676
>>62254644
Ah look the scammers still here even after mass reporting my thread lol, schizo scammer melty schizo melty lol fucking faggot I got your number, next your identity.
I got some faggots from /g/ working on it.
You just thought you were immune to exposure.
You're dead wrong and this time I want to see who you are.

62256655
>>62255610
Moron

62258565
>>62253066
For real?

62261255
>>62254644
Stay away Lebanon boy

62261309

62261638
>>62261309

item.title: NTDOY — 50 replies — 82.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
NTDOY

Open thread • Replies: 50 • Updated: 2026-05-22 10:53 UTC

OP

Currently Nintendo stock is extremely mispriced. All the short-term doom and gloom is priced in, ignoring the company's tremendous long-term upside. While it's not some 10x your money in a week meme coin, at this point it's a sleeper stock to hold you should know about.

Early replies (first 3)

62259388
>>62259371
its not mispriced. they make shitty games for 80+$. no one wants to buy their crap because its shit.

62259405
>NTDOY baggie
KWAB

62259407
>>62259371
not misspriced
no point in shilling your bags here btw
>>62259388
this and console era is coming to an end. Future will be only game stations with steam like platforms. retards still go for the ps5 by some sort of pavlovian reflex. it's just a standardized computer with a standardized controller. Nintendo shined for a while 30 years ago now it has become so cringe i can't stand their adds like wtf is even this shit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62259599
>>62259429
You are embarrassing. Take the L, shut Up and leave the discussion. Talk about stuff you know at least an ounce about.

62259722
>>62259526
>>62259566

62259729
>>62259371
My dad works here

62259742
>>62259729
Tell him the shareholders want him to work harder

62259746
I hope it works out for you bro but I don't want to fund what I see as a dying company in a dying market.

62259808
>>62259746
Mid 1980s: people predict video game market dead due to major crash and low-quality games
Late 1980s: people predict video game market dead due to arcade cooldown and home console "fad"
Mid 1990s: people predict video game market dead due to "CD Rom bubble panic" as commercial failures like Atari Jaguar and 3DO make people think industry is imploding
Early 2000s: people predict video game market dead due to 3D games becoming the norm and production budgets exploding
Mid 2000s: people predict video game market dead due to popularity of browser and web-based alternatives
Early 2010s: people predict video game market dead due to widespread popularity of mobile and social media gaming alternatives
Mid/Late 2010s: people predict video game market dead due to massive DLC, microtransactions, and "loot box" gambling dynamics
Early 2020s: people predict video game market dead due to "battle royale bubble" with high concentration in games like Fortnite
2026: random faggot on /biz/ predicts video game market dead because "this time it's different"

62259814
>>62259371
Switch 2 owner here. The new console sold very well due to the positive sentiment created by the Switch 1, and the hardware is certainly most pleasant to hold and see. Other than that, it’s a total disappointment.

>no games, the current lineup is both paltry and boring as fuck
>battery is kill after 2 hours
>storage on the console is rather low
>almost every 3rd party game is a fucking "game key card" that combines the worst of physical (having to carry a cartridge) and the worst of digital (takes the little storage you have)
>hardware self-destructs even if you take great care of it, my left joycon stick looks like it took 15 years of beatings after just a few months (it never happened with anything else I own)

Because of these problems, I’m buying 10 times fewer games on the Switch 2 compared to the Switch 1, and I will not buy a Switch 3 in the future given the more than mediocre treatment we get here.

TL;DR Nintendo gives the illusion of doing well with their numbers, but they’re actually shitting the bed. Disappointing performance will ensue if you are a long term shareholder.

62259828
>>62259814
The console is still in its infancy. Every criticism you have is fixable. New console-seller titles are inevitable. Imagine the Switch 2 Pro with battery and storage upgrades, etc.

62259884
>>62259808
Video games aren't dead. Consoles and reliance on proprietary IPs is. Gaming continues in phones for casuals and PC indies for the hardcore crowd. It's funny because had you invested in phones and social media instead of gaming companies since the early 2010s you'd be massively better off than an investment in Nintendo. Who's talking about "it'll be different this time"?

And anyway, yes, the world is different in 2026 than it was in 1980. Investment is about the future, not staring at previous success and insisting it'll happen again. What is Nintendo doing that could possibly replicate their boom in the 1980s?

62259900
>>62259828
It’s fixable, but will it be fixed? So far, nothing indicates that Nintendo takes the problem seriously, especially with the "no games" problem. Looks like Nintendo will just release four or five decent AAA games through the entire console’s lifespan (one Mario, one Zelda or whatever, and these are not even announced) and then call it a day.

62259983
>>62259371
Why would people be hungry for buying an ever increasing in price console (due to RAM) with zero new Zelda or Mario on it?

62260004
>>62259814
Dude, SW2 is a stop gap filler console until the real one (with a new hardware gimmick) wil be ready, the big games (mainline mario/zelda) will come out very late to be also used as launch titles for the next.
This is wii u/gba/3ds all over again.

62260032
>>62259371
>making this thread again
Please god nuke the third world

62260035
>>62259371
I agree. Not because of vidya, but because of Pokémon cards. The market cap of all PSA10 graded Pokémon cards is over $75B. Nintendo's market cap os only $53B. It makes no sense that the company that makes the cards is worth so much less than the cards themselves.

62260053
>>62260004
Nintendo could actually get away with it (releasing a Switch 3, 4, 5, etc. forever) by not being so retarded and underwhelming… Instead they will be forced to invent something else that may or may not be successful.

62260188
>>62259371
Thanks

62260304
>>62259371
agreed p/e is slurpable, nintendo aint going away

62260335
>>62259884
already priced in

62260390
Apple is apple

62260407
I bought a Rog ALLY X and it’s 100000x better than the switch I had for my son. You know the target audience? My kid uses the Ally to watch YouTube play actual fun games. He cares more about Spider-Man than bing bing wahoo slop. Nintendo failed to capture the audience by marketing to childless manchildren just like Disney Adults. They have no market share and sell an inferior product. Pokémon still looks and plays like an indie game from 2005. (lol)

Enjoy your bags tendie you get what you deserve.

62260606
>>62260407

62261314
Sorry about your hopes of pumping your bags OP, but I'll just leave this here...
>https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=luiKV1nHB1w

62261355
>>62259371
The switch 2 is overpriced. People who have money don't even know what it is. It also demotes the value of the switch one, which partially went subscription based model. So it was bad business design.

62261416
Tell me the last time they ever release a ground breaking software or hardware? The new pokemon is not a cashgrab.

62261518
anyway, you know when a retard here shill some stock:
1/ he is clueless no amount of shiling here will pump it not even 0.01%

2/therefore it's a bad pick

it's cringe.last retard shilled mc donald like it was some secret gem. The real blackpill is whatever gets shilled here don't buy it or even better sell it if you have some (pokemon cards, random crypto that miraculously pumped x10, nintengoy stocks, mc goynald stock, whatever)

62261624
>>62260035
>It makes no sense that the company that makes the cards is worth so much less than the cards themselves.
What psychogenic kush are you smoking? It makes a ton of sense. By your logic, a goldsmith can't have created a masterpiece work that he has sold 20 years ago but which is now recognized to be more valuably than his entire shop.

62261625
Value Trap 2: Electric Boogaloo

62261640
>>62259371
I agree. They are the one last big truly JP dev remaining, huge franchise value that's only getting better, the only one console with exclusives, ninety-something % of the serious handheld business with proper customer attachment, JP approach to quality as opposed to deadlines, no DEI, success with theme park now expanding, huge success with the films now.
No reason not to own their stock, I think it's a good 50% undervalued.

62261642
>2009
>Nintendo has suffered a drawdown of 50%
>ZOMG invest in Nintendo!!
>keeps dropping
>10 years to reach the position you entered

Yeah nah, I know a correction when I see it.

62261677
>>62261625
kek.
What is your average OP?
Buying this above 2 is like giving to a charity

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-05-22 11:29 UTC

OP

will probably buy an RV in europe and go travelling around the continent
my main goal is to eat tasty italian and french food
maybe buy a reasonable house ($2-3million GBP) 20 min from London

who else here has #madeit?

ngmi

62260612

62260626
put it in a hardware wallet, retard

62260631

62261694

Its a centralised shitcoin

62261718

Just be yourself and it will come back to you

62261721
>>62260563

62261725

'hot' wallets are actually better if your not a retard with clicking links

62261728

*lots
sorry guys im drunk

item.title: 16 years ago today Laszlo Hanyecz paid 10,000 BTC for 2 pizzas — 14 replies — 25.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
16 years ago today Laszlo Hanyecz paid 10,000 BTC for 2 pizzas

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-05-22 10:57 UTC

OP

Two pizzas aren’t on the table; they’re sitting at it. This was a deliberate act of rub, clearly signalling to the right crowd. BTW, where are these kids now? Because I can find his recent photos, but zero photos of *his* kids.

Early replies (first 3)

62261169
>>62261166
Still more valuable than anything else bitcoin has ever done

62261183
Why isn’t this diverse

62261194
>>62261183
ew

62261207

haha yeah wouldn't that be funny. lucky me even back then it was impossibly hard to mine so i have none

62261263
>>62261166
bro those kids are 20+ years old they don't live at home anymore dumb fuck, at that age parents get a picture or two a year if lucky. less if they live out of town.

62261274
>>62261263
That dad is 100% gay you can see it on his face and his faggy kids, similar to that jewish actor Sabretooth whos son was sucking on his bike handle like it was a sucker, now he's a famous trans femboy.

62261391

62261396

>creepy pic with two kids as evidence
Yeah, sure, and John Podesta has a diverse collection of handkerchiefs, righto

62261400
>>62261166
Bitcoin wouldn't be where its out without that purchase

62261517
>>62261263
>bro those kids are 20+ years old they don't live at home anymore dumb fuck
Alright, where is his wife then? There's zero photos of him and mother of *his* kids

62261678
>>62261391
Nice one Saul

62261685
>>62261166
child trafficking right there in front of everyone all in the name of monetary freedom

62261687
>>62261517
Not everyone broadcasts their life's online you zoomer fuck. There are literally zero images of me on the internet outside of some old Facebook friends from 10 years ago and I have been online since 95

item.title: Polymarket whales scam people through UMA shitcoin — 23 replies — 22.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polymarket whales scam people through UMA shitcoin

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-05-22 10:29 UTC

OP

If you've ever bet on Polymarket and assumed the oracle that resolves
your market is anything close to decentralized, read this.

Full breakdown video: [YOUR YOUTUBE LINK]

A $17M market — "Will Trump say 'Iran' during events with Xi Jinping?" —
is being resolved right now by 10 wallets holding 95.3% of voting power.
That's 4.2% of voters carrying nearly all the weight. Round closes within
hours. Source: betmoar.fun [image in reply]

The evidence the answer should be YES is not subtle:

>White House Press Pool Note #5: Trump on the record saying "We did
discuss Iran. We don't want them to have a nuclear weapon" at 11:55 AM
Beijing on May 15
>Fox News live YouTube stream — isLiveContent=true, broadcast window
10:57 AM to 12:22 PM Beijing — fully encloses the moment
>TVB Hong Kong live, was_live confirmed, stream start 11:56:11 Beijing
>Kalshi (CFTC-regulated) resolved the IDENTICAL contract YES, no dispute
>The "Peng" outcome in the same Polymarket market group already
resolved YES on functionally identical evidence 24h earlier

So how is the same evidence chain heading to NO on Polymarket?

>UMA.rocks — a "voting committee" that publicly announces its vote
(P1 / NO) in the dispute thread while including the disclaimer "we
encourage voters to vote independently." Delegators earn 20.84% APR
for voting with the committee, displayed in their own dashboard.
This is coordinated voting with a fig leaf — opposite of anonymous
token voting. [screenshots in reply]

>Polymarket's "Additional Context" mechanism — they added a
clarification AFTER the dispute opened, narrowing what counts as
"live broadcast" to exclude pool-feed-with-delay (i.e. the entire
Fox/TVB/AP chain). Users bet against the original rule. Polymarket
resolves against a retroactive interpretation.

>One UMA proposer address: 304 proposals, 100% win rate, 0 disputes
lost. Either the resolution flow is mathematically unloseable or
someone has structural edge ordinary users don't.

Early replies (first 3)

62257394
>>62257375
indeed, it is a massive scam

62257427
>>62257375
>Per market rules, "only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution." Footage that was not broadcast live, even if filmed during the relevant events and released during market timeframe, will not qualify toward resolution.

>We’re aware of the dispute on this market. If a clarification is to be issued, it will be at 1:00 PM ET on May 15. If no statement is issued at that time, then there will be no clarification by the Polymarket team. The orderbook will be cleared at 1:00 PM ET, regardless of whether a clarification is made.
Could it be that the rules were different and you did not read them?

62257430
>>62257394
>>62257427
>>62257375
kill yourself baldie.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62257458
>>62257430
Im not bald you retard. Not even a bit.

62257478
>Honduran presidential election — UMA dispute voter pushed P1 NO
with a fabricated turnout recalculation from raw papeletas data,
contradicting La Prensa, El Heraldo, the EU observation committee,
and Transparencia Electoral's final report all citing 60.19%
turnout. Vote was edited to YES only after Polymarket intervened
to correct it. UMA.rocks weight made this very nearly succeed

62257482
Did you read the problem?

62257543
https://youtu.be/rWBb5dgUgJk

62257572
The UMA is weak my brothers.....

62257574
I don't get these whales if this is true. Do they intend on killing the platform for a quick profit?

62257686
gamble stupid things receive stupid results

62257695
There is metadata evidence matching official white house notes. You want fucking money? yes is cheap. Whales only have 20 million worth of UMA, maybe less. When this gets extended again because the official UMA admin ADMITTED they resolved p1 as a misclick, be sure that everyone who is hedging and buying yes at 0.3c is going to have more money to buy uma than those sweating because they have 1.8 million shares at 99.7c average. Already been going on for a week, whats another few weeks to flip 20$ into 5000$? Just need more people to join. We can make a smart contract that works the same as uma.rocks and defeat the uma cartel

62257697
>>62257375
Yeah it's well known that polymarket is a scam. They've decided the opposite of reality in many cases already. The only realistic explanation is that they are profiting from it, otherwise they would have threw UMA in the trash years ago.

62257986
>>62257574
no shit? take the money & run is always the best thing to do when given the opportunity

62258009
>>62257375
why would you vote on what politicians are saying in a closed door meeting
fucking retard

62258469
Namefags deserve the rope

62258549
>>62257375
>[YOUR YOUTUBE LINK]
kek

62258593
>>62257375
Buy an ad.

62259240
>>62257375
Bump just so others start to notice

HOW MANY TIMES YOU DUMB FUCKS HAVE TO BE TOLD THAT POOlymarket is a fucking SCAM?

>2+2 is 4? = Y (off course)
>Uh un chuds, UMA whales said is 5 because..reasons and Einstein and watermelon science man theories said is 5 so get rekt and thanks for playing

UMA whales decide every outcome, it's not about facts but voting power and who has the biggest whales

62259257
>>62259240
no way dude, putting up thousands of dollars on bets for questions with esoteric rules and subjective results is really smart

62261306
>>62257375
>[YOUR YOUTUBE LINK]
You forgot to put in the link your (((handlers))) gave you. No goodgoy points for you from your tiny hat wearing masters.

62261313
>>62257375
>>62258549

62261332
>>62257375
>AI slop as OP pic
GTFO

62261653
Yes, I forgotten to paste our video link when was recompleting the captcha. But post originated from us, I only use Clause to make it shorter due to 2000 symbols. We just want fairness, nothing more

item.title: BREAKING: Ripple Joins Chainlink's SCALE Program — 10 replies — 3.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
BREAKING: Ripple Joins Chainlink's SCALE Program

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-05-22 08:53 UTC

OP

>Ripple is thrilled to announce its participation in Chainlink’s SCALE program, a significant step toward enhancing blockchain interoperability and fostering innovation in financial services.
>By joining SCALE, Chainlink will contribute to the Ripple ecosystem, facilitating seamless access to real-time data and off-chain computation for smart contracts.
>Ripple’s recently launched stablecoin, $RLUSD, will also benefit from enhanced oracle connectivity, enabling greater transparency and reliability for its use in payment and settlement solutions within the TradFi ecosystem.

https://chainlinktoday.com/ripple-joins-chainlink-scale-with-all-chainlink-services-live-on-mainnet/

https://x.com/Ripple/status/1869461289273615724

Early replies (first 3)

62257616
Boom status?

62257648
>>62257595
DOUBLE BOOOOOOM

WE'RE FEELING EXTRA CUUCKY TODAY

62257663
>>62257595
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62257951
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62257982
>>62257595
none of those links work

62258140
So Ripple will be paying millions to Chainlink to interact with their services and not the other way around? Damn thank you Xerpies

62258143
{: pain and misery :}

62258182
>>62257616
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kZzBd41NuZw

62261499
>>62258140
Yet zero for token holders who continue to decline and always will.

62261515

item.title: American Reserve Modernization Act of 2026 — 3 replies — 3.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
American Reserve Modernization Act of 2026

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-05-22 10:29 UTC

OP

The US Bitcoin Strategic Reserve will be the new Fort Knox.
ARMA is being drafted, and a major announcement will soon ignite the Bitcoin race.
I hope you're not waiting to buy until October.

Early replies (first 3)

62260596
>>62260467
that would be quite the exit scam

62260630
>>62260596
>200,000 btc bought each year for 5 years
>20 year holding requirement
That's an exit scam how?

62261654
The American Reserve Modernization Act (ARMA) would authorize the U.S. Treasury to acquire up to 200,000 bitcoin per year for five years, targeting a reserve of 1 million BTC.

The bill was introduced Wednesday by Rep. Nick Begich with 16 original cosponsors. It is a rebranding and expansion of the original BITCOIN Act co-introduced last year by Begich and Senator Cynthia Lummis. It would codify Trump's March 2025 executive order establishing a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve into permanent law that cannot be reversed by a future president.

The bill classifies bitcoin as a "Tier 1" strategic reserve asset, putting it on the same legal footing as gold. A separate digital asset stockpile would hold other federally owned crypto.

All acquisitions must be budget-neutral. The funding mechanism is revaluing Federal Reserve gold certificates from their current statutory price of $42.22 per ounce, set in 1973, to current market prices. That gap generates hundreds of billions in accounting gains without new taxpayer debt.

The bill also ends the practice of auctioning off seized bitcoin. All future seizures would be transferred directly to the Strategic Reserve instead of being liquidated by the U.S. Marshals Service. Existing government holdings would be consolidated into a single audited ledger.

Bitcoin in the reserve would be held for a minimum of 20 years. The bill establishes federal custody standards including geographic distribution of private keys across air-gapped facilities, multi-signature governance requiring authorization from the Treasury, the Fed, and an independent third agency, and investment in "quantum-resistant cryptographic upgrades."

Rep. Pat Harrigan, one of the cosponsors, said "The United States government already holds billions in seized bitcoin with no coherent strategy for managing it, and that needs to change."

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn… — 6 replies — 2.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn…

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-05-22 08:50 UTC

OP

A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn $1 per 1 LINK per DAY through staking. Does that mean if I’ve staked 2000 LINK, I’d earn $2000 per day?
Is that true, or was the anon just talking bullshit?

Early replies (first 3)

62258684
complete bullshit. if they opened staking to everyone and diverted 100% of all revenue to stakers(never happening), each link would make you less than a penny a day.

good thing holders are retarded and you can just make up bullshit for a decade tho

62258761
yes, but a loaf of bread will also cost 2000

62259325
test

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62259833
>>62258598
>sergei will deny again

62259895
>>62258598
2 in the pink, 1 in the LINK

62261509
>>62258598
Yes my friend your math is not a challenge. It is to be this for us and to every one of our communities so good and wealth will be extreme. We will to be in good spirits and also very full with lots of food and enjoyment for our families and lives! Go go gadget LINK!!!!

item.title: HODL and buy more BTC. — 71 replies — 58.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
HODL and buy more BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 71 • Updated: 2026-05-22 10:17 UTC

OP

If you still don’t own any Bitcoin yet, anon what the hell are you waiting for?

>Banks give you tiny interest while inflation slowly destroys your purchasing power anyway. I’d rather have BTC long term than let my money rot in a savings account.

Early replies (first 3)

62245757
i get 5btc later for cheaper but thanks for the advice!

62245760
>>62245741
Too eqrly but yeah and avoid cex or at least take it out as fast as u can!

62246438
>>62245757
How much BTC are you holding right now anon?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62257223
>>62245741
fact, he's still hodling

62257253
>>62257223
Is he satoshit?

62257345
>>62257173
ICp? Lol

62257392
>>62257204
but everything in defi is hype

62257440
>>62257223

62257675
>>62245741
>Banks give you tiny interest while inflation slowly destroys your purchasing power anyway.
BTC doesn't improve any of this. Its cheaper and easier to spend gold.

62257679
>>62246878
It was decided that BTC would never scale. Self custody is impossible on BTC. Go ahead and try it. I swear when I make it I am going to hunt small blockers with a rifle.

62257680
it's boomer coin

62258573
>>62257679
will your cut your d?

62258577
>>62257680
you have zero bitcoin

62258754
>>62257223
Where is he now

62258775
>>62257679
nonsense. It's all in your head

62258793
>>62258754

62259397
>>62245757
lets hold hands and buy in Oct

62259745
>>62259397
What's happening in October?

62260090
>>62259745
He’s buying

62261050
>>62257679
BTC infrastructure is basically trying to solve that balance now: preserving self custody while making native BTC more usable across broader financial systems. Maybe you’re just not updated yet.

62261057
>>62252472
Buy btc for many reasons anon.

62261131
>>62257675
*sends 5 million dollars worth of gold across the world in 5 minutes for a nominal fee*

62261260
>>62261050
It's kinda scary to go deeper with that
>>62261131
Plus big chance for theft

62261264
>>62258793
Hello I'm here

62261340
>>62257392
>>62261260
Nothing wrong with being cautious, just don’t treat all BTCFi the same. Big names like a16zcrypto, Ledger, GoMining and others wouldn’t even touch it if everything was complete garbage.

62261342
>>62252488
50k? Nooo way

62261452
>>62260090
100k by October

62261462
>>62261452
no chance
this will count as the summer of the big dump

62261476
>>62261462
Your opinion doesn't matter to me

62261608
>>62261340
Why do people still not know about this?
Better to explore and understand the TBV direction early before launch.

62261618
>>62261462
How do you know?

62261632
>>62252488
Poor mindset

62261641
>>62261131
Fee: $20000

item.title: FIRE is mental illness — 129 replies — 44.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
FIRE is mental illness

Open thread • Replies: 129 • Updated: 2026-05-22 08:35 UTC

OP

No woman, no kids, $25k YEARLY spend.
You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing. What is wrong with modern men?

Early replies (first 3)

62259938
>>62259935
Keep slaving away goyman

62259945
>>62259935
Nice bait thread faggot

62259968
>>62259935
You can't really afford to marry on FIRE because one divorce ruins a decade+ of saving and then you have to work again. If a woman finds out you're FIRE she will absolutely divorce rape you too since the wealth disparity between you and her is so great she'll walk away with a ludicrous amount of money.

Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62261053
>>62261016
That's entirely state dependent. If you bought another house in my state during marriage then auto its her's. What's really fucked is if some states target your pension/401k ect.

62261093
>>62260570
My dad has started nagging me about getting married and giving him grandkids like he wasn’t an abusive piece of shit my whole life that actively tried to sabotage me until I finally escaped the house. His marriage to my mom was also a nightmare plus he cheated on her. I’d cut him off completely, but I want my inheritance.

Boomers never stop booming, man.

62261104
>>62260803
>You could tell her, I’m down to marry but I’m putting 80% of that $1.9 million into gold coins. And I’m going to bury it somewhere that you will never find. And if you divorce me I will kms and nobody will ever find it. But if you don’t divorce me and let me fuck you whenever I want no matter if you are sick of me or not you will be protected until the day we both die because that 1.5 million in gold will last us till the end
Gonna be funny when she records this and plays it back to you in court so she can take everything you have.
Biztards always think they got it all figured out. As if all the divorced men out there just weren’t as smart as you. Tom Brady? What does that loser know, amiright?

62261112
>>62261104
Imagine being Tom Brady and your bitch post wall wife thinks she is on your level or above.

62261114
>>62260817
At my private Christian highschool, we had this 50 year old, obese, incel science teacher that said he was “Waiting for marriage.” I felt bad for him because I knew his bride was never coming.

But we also had an obese, female science teacher that was somehow married to a 6’4”, jacked Navy Chad. That perplexed me. I couldn’t understand why the obese woman had a Chad husband while the obese man had nobody.

I understand now. The world is fucked.

62261118
>>62260570
nice story Chang-anon
get another job and you will be fine, you have the hard work never stop gene
you are living the real life

62261123
>>62261114
Navy guys make due anon. For him it was a 50/50 chance of getting married to a filipino or a fat bitch.

62261125
>>62261114
>obese, female science teacher that was somehow married to a 6’4”, jacked Navy Chad
No mystery there

62261126
>>62259935
Eh, I get it. I am also a chill guy who could garden and cook and play video games and watch TV and be happy.
But I have 4 kids and the youngest is 1 so I'm never ever retiring.
Oh well.

62261134
>>62260927
>All i want is just blow job once a day is it too much to ask for?
I personally love eating pussy. I figured I’d find a girl that enjoys sucking dick as much as I like eating pussy.

But that doesn’t seem to be the case. I dated a girl for like 2 years, and got less than 100 blowjobs over those 2 years. I’ve only ever met 1 woman who enjoyed giving blowjobs, but it didn’t work out for other reasons.

I eventually gave up on marriage, and that was part of the reason. Weirdly enough, the best blowjobs I’ve ever gotten were from femboys. Because femboys actually love sucking dick, in a way a woman never could.

I guess that’s part of the issue in society. Women just don’t like dick all that much. At least not in the way men like pussy.

Anyways, marriage always seemed like a bum deal for men. A lot of dudes don’t even fuck their wives. What’s the point?

62261135
>>62260263
The "jewish ghettos" throughout Europe are always the richest and best located parts of every European city center mere steps from all the palaces and seats of power.

62261144
>>62261033
You’ve never been to prison/jail, faggot. You wouldn’t last a day. You ready for Jerome to shove his cock up your ass? You ready for the guards to laugh at you when you report it?

My ex-girlfriend’s dad was a prison guard. Prison sucks. The only reason to comply to this gay ass fagsociety is because prison sucks so bad. It’s somehow worse outside the USA.

62261145
>>62260376
This is so unhinged, seriously can someone explain the artists lack of hinges

62261211
>>62260748
Having children is the only way to signal to them that you're not a afraid and won't go quietly into the night

62261216
>>62261123
guys in the military always have the ugliest wives

62261238
>>62261216
I can't argue against that i only ever saw officers with hot wives and the officers were known swingers.

62261282
>>62260635
that you think having a wife and kids is better has nothing to do with anything, it's just societal pressure to conform, so many people can't seem to understand this, they are just being led along to make the most stupid decisions and the funny thing is that you can't blame society for these decisions, but society can shame you when you don't make them, its the biggest iq filter, society mostly being roasties and miserable married men

62261285
>>62261211
Lmao required virtue signalling to do something effortless

62261290
>>62261104
Notice how I said I’d kms

62261296
>>62261104
Doesn’t matter if Tom Brady gets raped for half his net worth cuz he’ll still have well over 100 million dollars in the bank and can easily make plenty more.

Also the OP isn’t dating now. So if I’m sitting on 2 million and I’m thinking to myself maybe I will get married at some point. That’s plenty of time to maneuver and get a nice gold stash hidden.

62261311
buy defi tokens
stake eth
LP bitcoin
make a secret XMR stash
don't pay taxes
don't pay for goyslop
don't watch their media
don't use their services
don't give them your time
don't work for them
don't give them any peace

62261325
>>62259935
Nope you need at least $2.5mm to think about retiring with those expenses + $1mm (40%) to safeguard against inflation

62261335
>>62259935
>You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing.

Yeah, youre supposed to wage until you cant and have nothing.

62261370
>>62260945
i fucking burst out laughing when i finished reading your post

62261381
>>62260803
Would you still kill yourself if she won wheel of fortune and divorced?

62261403
>>62259938
>just rot in you goyburb for 45 years until you die alone

62261427
>>62261134
100 blow jobs in 2 years is actually some kind of record. Even the best blow job queen i ever met preferred to have her pussy fucked than to blow me.

62261430
>>62261104
Didn't his wife take his tens of millions then start fucking her yoga instructor within days?

62261451
This needs to be known as incelFIRE, lonely guys who just want to check out of society and working and live in their cabin in the woods.

62261480
>>62261451
Its impossible to do if you want your kids setup for the future. None of these people talk about private school/college/tutoring and extracurriculars. I would gladly go into debt for that shit if i had kids. these people just want to rot until inflation catches up to them. In which case they need to add 40% to their current projected goal anyways.

item.title: I hold thousands of LINK and regularly spread Chainlink FUD — 23 replies — 11.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
I hold thousands of LINK and regularly spread Chainlink FUD

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-05-22 09:32 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62257369
>>62257367
you should have sold 5 years ago, hell I sold in 2022 (at $13) and got called retarded by the cult but it turned out to be a fantastic decision.

62257370
>>62257369
>you should have sold 5 years ago
>he doesn't know

62257380
>>62257370
you're right it was 6 years ago to be exact, the peak was August 2020. I still remember those days of pure euphoria mooning against the entire market.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62257498
>>62257380
>he still doesn't know

62257507
>>62257367
don't we all
still buying to this day

62257511
>>62257369
>>62257370
>>62257380
>>62257498
>>62257507

KAAABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOMMMMM!!!!!!!!

62258063
>>62257498
doesnt know what, stinkie? are you implying link is gonna le mooooon to $1000 soon?
cuz if so, eat shit
eat literal excrement

62258569
>>62257367
One day you'll wake up and the realization of all that wasted time and cost opportunity spent on post ironic memes will hit you all at once and you'll blow your brains out. Along with every other bagholder who killed themselves as well

62258599
>>62257367
I have 60k Link staked in the community pool and 23k not staked. I'll be staking those when the pool expands. Also I have 629K SXT staked and 139K FHE staked, that I got for nothing from Build rewards.
I partly (though by no means fully) have fudders to thank for my diamond hands. It was abundantly clear from their obsession that Link was the one. And here we are. Nearly there.

62259006
>>62258599
83k LINK is a fat stack. Why did you not sell at $50? Could have bought literally any blue chip stock and have $10M+ by now. Anyway, I'm jealous. I have worked up to just over 60k now, though I had way less back then or I definitely would have sold.

62259041
>>62259006
Why did (You) not sell at $50?
And then bought vaccine stocks and sold the top?
And then bought oil futures and sold the top?
And then bought NVDA and sold the top?
Or even better, SanDisk?

62259090
>>62258599
>Nearly there.
nearly at the poorhouse, yes stinkie
the dump to zero is gonna be so sweet

62259157
>>62259041
No no no linky, you don't get to use those standards.

Why didn't YOU use you're foresight to perfectly buy the bottom and perfectly sell the top

62259345
>>62259006
>Why did you not sell at $50?
Because the pool is closed.

62260007
>>62259157
Just know the future bro just buy every bottom sell every top Bro its simple

62260100
>>62259345
because the cope is cope
fixed that for u, stankie

62260833
I have a large stack of Link and wont be selling at $50 the next time, either. Imagine kek

62260933
>>62260833
but if you did you would be rich right now
you lost the game of life, stinkbat
it's ogre

62261383
>>62260933
>you would be rich right now
and i'm not? trouble with small minded ppl is you're always unable to think bigger than your own world

62261432
>>62261383
You’re a cuckold who married his 2017 shitcoin bags. Have fun missing another bullrun in 2028

62261460
>>62257367
Me too. And I used to harass Sergey Nazarov on Twitter almost daily from 2019 to 2022.

62261485
>>62261432
Wow. This guy has wasted a good portion of his life sitting here typing this shit.
Anyway, I'm out. Was nice to revisit after an abscence, but I now remember why I left.

62261589
>>62257367
I have only a ~5k stack (staked), bought most of it 2018 when I was still in school and Link 20 cents. Then went to university and only last year started my carreer and getting a regular wage.
Thanks for keeping the price down so I can finally accumulate more.

item.title: Blackpill me on AI — 26 replies — 11.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Blackpill me on AI

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-05-22 09:14 UTC

OP

Are people going to be in UBI camps in a few years? Are all these people losing their jobs in tech fucked fucked? Or just fucked and can do freelance work or whatever.

Early replies (first 3)

62261294
AI is just a chatbot

62261308
>>62261280
You need to invest in the police state. We will have to keep the goyim under an iron boot. PLTR, GEO, LMT, etc

62261334
>>62261280
muh apocalypse. nothing ever happens retard, or atleast not in the way you expect. yea there will be more neets (oh no's anything but my goyim job) and a slow transition into more efficient systems (its not eating bugs)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62261339
>>62261294
This.

Make another hundred threads about it

62261346
>>62261280
It's always fun to do that schizophrenic thing where you imagine there's actually someone rational in charge and they have a solid plan that they'll carry out with no mistakes

but back in the real world nobody smart is in charge. There is no plan. And if there was a plan nobody is competent to carry it out anyways.

what's going to happen is more and more people are going on government assistance or moving to countries where AI isn't a thing yet. It's possible this will cause the collapse of western economies but there's no real guarantee. Even if they do collapse, something else will take their place. Probably drugs and hookers.

62261354
>>62261346
stop being mean, most normies believe it will all work out and that's enough to turn your brain off and hope for the best, i mean what's the alternative, right, ai is just a statistical churner, what's the worst that could happen

62261360
>>62261346
moron, just like corona collapsed everything right? oh wait tons of people got rich and life was great. say it with me, nothing ever happens

62261372
>>62261360
for about 7 million people covid was literally the end of the world.

AI isn't anything like that. It's just going to cause a lot of unemployment for people doing jobs that probably weren't necessary in the first place.

unfortunately those same people are the ones making most of the money.

62261378
>>62261372
as far as im concerned 7 million die a year from obesity. woopty do.

62261388
>>62261378
yep, and for them it's the end of the world.

suffering is relative. Somebody's world is always ending. I doubt AI is going to kill anyone or end mankind, but it's probably capable of disrupting the economy you're used to. Maybe ending it.

62261390
>>62261280
Nothing ever happens

62261392
>>62261388
We're talking about major collapse, not individual lives being lost??????

62261404
>>62261392
If you don't think covid was a major collapse we're not really talking about anything.

Just like the 2008 collapse. The economy never actually recovered from either of those but since it didn't change your life much you ignore the permanent damage done. But it adds up.

Young people bitch constantly about how unaffordable everything is and blame boomers. But boomers know exactly when this stuff happened. During collapses you pretend didn't happen

62261412
>>62261404
yea because in 2015 everything was affordable.

62261419
>>62261412
It was much better

things go to shit a little bit at a time, but some events speed it up a lot.

overall things are getting worse for the working class, and every financial collapse has sped that up. Not my problem, but eventually enough of you guys will be unemployed and homeless to do something about it. AI will presumably speed that up a lot

main problem being almost the entire US economy is service-based, and the vast majority of services we provide can be done by AI.

62261424
the blackpill is that no one knows what they are doing, it's all trial and error all the way from the govt to the ai labs and every decision they make or fail to make is potentially going to permanently damage things, if you do physics or engineering, we are currently in an unstable regime and equilibrium for this sort of thing has never been established before so there's no going back to normal because we won't know what that looks like anymore, it's like landing on an alien planet, you don't know anything about anything and lots of your colleagues are going to die from making stupid mistakes

62261433
>>62261419
Yea the topic is not overall or bit by bit, the topic is major collapse, sweaty. You can say its been down hill since the 60s doesnt make it a "happening"

62261438
>>62261433
>You can say its been down hill since the 60s doesnt make it a "happening"
It hasn't though

things were pretty steady and good right up to the early 2000's. 2008 really fucked it up for you guys, and covid made it even worse.

your mistake is thinking the collapse is a single fast event. In reality you have the single event and then 5 years of shit fallout after.

we are already in the AI collapse, you just won't know it until decades later. You might never realize it if you're not very smart.

62261447
>>62261438
k

62261453
>>62261447
thanks for reading my blog

62261455
>>62261280
Try asking AI about anything you're knowledgeable about and you'll quickly find that it's completely retarded. AI isn't replacing shit.

62261463
>>62261455
I'm not sure, but the way I understand it the AI they're working with is not the same AI the public gets for free. Is this wrong?

even if it is the same, we're seeing it improve in real time. When it's wrong it's almost always because it's finding wrong information on the internet. Which should be an easy problem to fix if humans decide to curate what it's trained on.

62261492
>>62261455
This is a good thing btw. We don't want the goyim to know.

62261531
>>62261463
>When it's wrong it's almost always because it's finding wrong information on the internet.
you can usually tell, and without pay are limited in how often you can "go online"
Your close but you need to understand the issue isn't "oh little guy just pulling up the wrong info " it's "Word algorithm used a instead of an in an internal scratchpad that you can't even see and has "cheerflly persona"d the results to you so convincing that unless you can tell that .01% mistake has now compounded to easily 5%+ visible in the reply".
Do you not understand anything above midwit level so can't test this yourself?

62261543
>>62261531
I see it fuck up all the time. I just doubt the federal government and major corporations are using the google AI to spy on people and engage in social engineering. Maybe they are, I don't know. But I doubt it.

62261558
>>62261543
most certainly a fake panopticon as a threat imo. Look at the business success of implementation. Not possible?
The issue i see with it is its conference. If someone figures out human level "queston yourself constantly" id be curious but atm it whole heartedly goes with it's own conclusion.

on a far more basic level , baidu/ernie is at like 2020 jailbreak ease level. Shameful.

item.title: >be a normie >complain about rising costs of living >bitch about billionaires >*unmask the balance sheet* >12%+ frivolou… — 83 replies — 9.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
>be a normie >complain about rising costs of living >bitch about billionaires >*unmask the balance sheet* >12%+ frivolou…

Open thread • Replies: 83 • Updated: 2026-05-22 02:25 UTC

OP

>be a normie
>complain about rising costs of living
>bitch about billionaires
>*unmask the balance sheet*
>12%+ frivolous fees/month
Why can't normies budget? is it just low IQ? Like this Plebbitor spends $110 a month on completely frivolous expenses at his stage (haircuts, spotify)

Also stories from normies being shit with money.

Early replies (first 3)

62254520
>take on debt to go to university
>make $2,200 a month
lol lmao

62254533
>>62254481
How is it fucking possible to waste $96 on a fucking haircut a month? And $13 for a fucking spotify? Aren't there free unlockers which take like 3 minutes to install?

62254543
>retarded normie can't help hemorrhaging money on dumb bullshit
A tale as old as time. He could get rid of his gym membership and with just a couple months of savings buy dumbbells and a pullup bar. He can buy a $20 electric razor and just shave his head a couple times a month. Music is free, if you pay for it you're a dumbass nigger.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62256572
>living in current day in america is like living in a third world country! Slavery never went away, it just evolved!
>ORDERS 12 DOORDASHES AND DAILY STARBUCKS

62256579
>>62256544
How do I budget for exponential never ending inflation on my stagnant wage, dumb fucking cocksucker?

62256585
>>62256544
bro, anon, i love you, but stop condoning this shit already

62256781
>>62256585
He’s right. The system is fucked but most of the world would kill for $2500 a month and it’s absolutely enough if you can cook chicken and potatoes and not spend money like a woman

62256785
LLM thread lol 50 1pbitd

62256791
>>62254551
>>62256396
>I live in DC
>MM
Nice larp ranjesh now go cover yourself in jam for me boy.

62256797
>>62256791
>for you ESL's confused by this nobody in the history of american history has ever abbreviated 20m (20 million)
with 20mm
It isn't a socket you ESL faggots, now please please if you're gonna LLM spam and larp goto r9k

62256815
>>62256797
You’re not nearly as smart as you think you are. Take a deep breath

62256858
>>62256815
Yea okay Bybon, son of Phola

62256865
>>62254481
I spend 5$/month for my phone. If I go over data it just slows down a bit but I don't get charged more. I'm mostly on wifi anyway. Who pays $110/mo for a phone bill?

62256883
>>62254481
Squat rack, bench and weights pays for itself in 6 months but maybe he doesn't have the space so okay whatever. $50 haircut every two weeks idk maybe he's black and needs a slick fade or the homies will jump his ass. Seems like a waste o money when you have $0 in the bank and a razor would pay for itself in two weeks

62257079
>>62256781
Most of the world aren't relevant since they aren't living in America

62257084
>>62254802
>Normies are called cattle by Jews and other non-normies for a reason.
Cause their parents don't buy them a block of flats with tenants once they turn 18?

62257246
>>62256883
>$50 haircut every two weeks idk maybe he's black and needs a slick fade or the homies will jump his ass.
imagine trying to justify being a nigger

62257421
>>62255083
>The median net worth of most people in america age 25-35 is probably closer to 3-5K USD
be realistic

62257449
>>62256865
He doesn’t own his phone, so he’s paying some company like Verizon or T-Mobile $110 a month instead of buying it outright.
Normies are fucking retarded.

62257993
>>62256523
>$15 to wash your clothes at the laundromat???
lol the one I got to is $16 total actually, $8 to wash and $8 to dry but it is the only one within walking distance and i dont have a car. it's all white people here so they can get away with those kinds of prices

62258026
>>62254481
>$110 for a phone. Does this faggot pay for triple platinum 6G with 10Gbit up/down and free calls to Nauru and Tuvalu?
What the fuck?

62258176
>>62254481
>rent $425
Is this from 2010?

62258317
>>62255552
Bro if you weren't so midwit yourself you'd read that my post grants their IQ went down. Because it literally couldn't have done anything but gone down. I'm not arguing it didn't go down.

62258334
Thinly veiled reddit ad

Kill yourself

62258471
>>62254481
>Phone - 110
Faggot shit. I pay $39/month on T-mobile 12GB Max Data Prepaid (just turn it to WiFi mode whenever home)
>Bus - 30
Get a bike
>Gym - 75
Buy some free weights, bench, and rack from Craiglist
>Haircut x2 - 96
$50 haircuts twice a month? WTF?
>Spotify - 13
Learn to torrent
>Monthly Income - 2,200
This nigger is only making $26.4k a month? Even just the most basic low level security / receptionist job pays $40k per year!

What a faggot. OP image taker should KYS.

62258608
>>62256781
pic related. nobody wants to hear a peep from anarcho-capitalist retards and rabbis on this topic. you can't keep spinning the prices going up like this.

>>62257993
yeah i figured. i thought my example was hyperbolic. and you're just confirming it's already gotten worse than that. it's completely ridiculous, my example was already obsoleted.
> it's all white people here so they can get away with those kinds of prices
no, they really can't. that shit is dumb.

62260577
>>62256797
>I'm poor
>my blockfolio never showed MM

62260603
>>62254481
>monthly income: 2200
>next check: 1250
math aint mathing

62260614
>>62258608
Boomers built and put in place the system that gave them 25 cent cheeseburger meals. Zoomers were raised on the internet and subsequently expect the world to be handed to them without doing any work.
tl;dr zoomers are lazy crybabies.

62260618
>>62254543
>have 36,293 in debt
>cancel gym membership, sportify, stop getting haircuts and start shaving your head
literally everyone can tell that you cut your own hair, by the way
there is never a situation where it doesnt look like shit
anyway
>save 184/month
With this incredible advice, he'll be able to pay off his debt in a little over 16 years, assuming it's not accumulating interest (it is).

You're on the same level of retardation as boomers declaring that young people can't afford homes because they're spending all their money on avocado toast. Going to the gym and getting professional haircuts to not look like a sperg who cuts his own hair will unironically be a far better financial investment than saving a couple hundred dollars a month. Being fit and well groomed will go a long way toward getting a better job which is the only realistic advice you should be giving someone like this, not fucking
>well if you just cut your own hair for the next 20 years then you might save enough to pay down your debt

62260655
>>62254481
>Utility - $150
This can likely be cut 20% if you use heat/AC at a moderately more uncomfortable level.
>Gym - $75
I'm a gym rat myself but running and pushups are FREE!
>Haircut x2 - $96
Buy $20 clippers and cut your own hair.
>Spotify - $13
Spotify is free with ads.

Boom! I just saved this little faggot at least $200 a month. I won't touch on the ridiculous phone bill since I assume that's locked into a contract.

62260660
>>62254481
you get distracted by those things instead of understanding that you will never be as wealthy as those at the top. the game is rigged and you will never get there even if you don't spend on anything at all

62260740
>>62260614
Poor bait

item.title: Anon, its my bday today Im in early 30s, close to no friends, no gf (ditched a bitch around 2 years ago), not much in th… — 16 replies — 8.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Anon, its my bday today Im in early 30s, close to no friends, no gf (ditched a bitch around 2 years ago), not much in th…

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-05-22 09:32 UTC

OP

Anon, its my bday today
Im in early 30s, close to no friends, no gf (ditched a bitch around 2 years ago), not much in the bank, had a successful career but then pivoted to make a startup and lost shit ton of money and time in the process. Not sure what to do anymore. Basically going through the dark night of the soul.

Now I'm here trying to find a tech job in a fucking US to not die on the street. You are, as usual, my only real friend.

Tell me something nice or not.

Early replies (first 3)

62261420
>>62261414
>Now I'm here trying to find a tech job in a fucking US
Are you a foreigner? Is this a LARP?

62261422
You did good

62261428
>>62261414
Your life can turn around in a week easy. New job, new girl, market pumps, whatever. Don't be so down anon you don't know what is around the corner. Also you're only early 30s, you're young as fuck, you have to live as long as you already have all over again before you hit mid 60s, just think how long that truly is. Stop worrying and enjoy your life you fag.

Happy Birthday, enjoy the magic trick.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62261442
>>62261428
whoops wrong file

62261445
Doing way better than I am

62261472
>>62261420
yes im from EU

62261490
>>62261428
thanks anon, i will try my best, there is nothing to do for me anyway

62261501
>>62261414
the answer lies at the end of a shotgun barrel.

You know this, i know this, we all know this yet we cling to life like ticks

62261504
>>62261472
Go back to your country

62261512
>>62261504
I really don’t understand why an euro would want to move to USA these days. T. An euro

62261523
>>62261512
not being paid 20 euros per hour max?

62261539
>>62261512
We have much lower barriers to starting a business or making money.

other than that, yeah. US isn't having a great time right now.

62261551
>>62261539
>We have much lower barriers to starting a business

I don’t know if this was always a myth, but at least nowdays in many european countries it’s just as easy. The only reason why europeans don’t is because we don’t need to thanks to the welfare.

>>62261523
Unless you really luck out, all the extra income goes towards things europeans get for free.

62261563
>>62261551
really doubt it. Engineers here make 2-3k /month they can get 150k in the us. Some people get much more. That's commie cope

62261586
>>62261563
In my country they make 3k at start and over 4k easily, and of course they can get bigger salaries too. It’s a small number of engineers who get 150k in US. Overall there really isn’t an improvement in life quality, and you would have to leave all your friends and family.

62261587
>>62261414
Happy birthday anon. The world is unfair and many who have been successful are finding themselves in dire straits due to how awful everything is.
I think there is always the chance for things to the upside and have faith in you. Good luck.

item.title: LINK/BTC is trading at October 2017 levels — 14 replies — 3.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
LINK/BTC is trading at October 2017 levels

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-05-22 07:42 UTC

OP

it's also down -95% from the 2020 peak. And Linkies have the audacity to get mad when they get called out as cuckold baggies lol

Early replies (first 3)

62257365
>>62257363
more baldie cope lol all you do is spam all day
>MY LINK MY KIDNEYS
kill yourself

62257371
linkies will tell you "buy the dip"

62257386
>>62257371
>linkies will tell you "buy the dip"

ok i buy then a zero

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62257405
so ur saying it is now a good time to buy?

62257411
>>62257405
yeah dude, buy as much as you can! The Jews don't want you to buy Chainlink bro it's a conspiracy or something like that

62257417
>>62257363
Works on my machine.

62257426
>>62257417
what a wonderful PnD shitcoin chart keeek

62257429
>>62257411
remember when we were all making fun of xrp for being down like 99% vs btc and then it went to perform the best at the end of 24/start of 25

62258028
pain and misery, pain and pain and misery
pain and misery, pain and pain and misery
:}

62258103
stinkie linkies

62259296
THE cuckolds of crypto

62259320
>a 12x in gwei (the benchmark for alts) is le bad!!!

62261413
>>62259320
>zero profits in 8 years is actually….LE GOOD
shoot all cuckold shills
